text: Surgical management of functioning paragangliomas is a challenge, especially when they involve renal vascular structures, and are metabolically active. This video demonstrates a complex renal hilar paraganglioma, managed with robot-assisted excision, requiring reconstruction of renal vein and artery. Forty-two-year-old woman was diagnosed with left-sided hilar paraganglioma during evaluation of recent onset episodic hypertension. Twenty-four hours urinary metanephrines, and serum chromogranin A, were raised.

The surgeon performs robotic excision of the paraganglioma mass in the right lateral position, using multiple ports for access. The colon is mobilized to expose the renal hilum, revealing the mass situated between the renal vessels. The lower part of the mass is targeted for dissection, with the aortic border cleared to facilitate potential clipping of the renal vessels if necessary. The tumor appears adherent to the renal vessels and is highly vascular.

The surgeon finds the mass adherent to both the renal vein and artery during dissection. The renal vessels are clamped to facilitate safe dissection. An injury to the renal artery occurs while dissecting the tumor, leading to the decision to excise a segment of the renal vein along with the mass.

The surgeon performs an end-to-end anastomosis of the renal vein using Gore-Tex CV-VI suture in a continuous fashion. The renal artery is similarly repaired with Gore-Tex CV-Six suture. Hilar clamps are removed, and the excised specimen is placed in a bag. A drain is inserted, and the left kidney is preserved despite the tumor's adherence to the renal vessels.
